name: TREACHER-COLLINS SYNDROME
definition: MANDIBULOFACIAL DYSOSTOSIS autosomal dominant disease (with new mutations in 60%) characterized by bilateral
distribution: egg-shaped orbits = drooping of outer inferior orbital rim
discriminating_features: narrowing of retropharyngeal space (apnea, speech difficulties)
egg-shaped orbits = drooping of outer inferior orbital rim
hypoplasia of lateral wall of orbits + shallow / incomplete orbital floor

general: narrowing of retropharyngeal space (apnea, speech difficulties)
Eyes: egg-shaped orbits = drooping of outer inferior orbital rim
hypoplasia of lateral wall of orbits + shallow / incomplete orbital floor
Malar bone: sunken cheek ← marked hypoplasia / agenesis of zygomatic arches (= malar hypoplasia)
Mandible: pronounced micrognathia = mandibular hypoplasia with broad concave curve on lower
Ear: microtia with small middle ear cavity
atresia / stenosis of external auditory canal

clinical.Prognosis: early respiratory problems (tongue relatively too large for hypoplastic mandible)
